decagon_as_rectangle_generator.concept: A decagon can generate a meaningful rectangle when selecting specific vertex pairs.

details.major_side: Corresponds to the second diagonal d2 of the decagon.
details.minor_side: Corresponds to the radius r of the decagon.
details.projection_constraints: This correspondence is not universal for arbitrary projections.
details.valid_projections: There exist specific projection planes of the icosahedron where projected arcs and diagonals coincide with the decagon’s diagonals and radius.
details.hidden_decagons: These special planes are precisely those that contain the 'hidden' decagons embedded in the icosahedron’s symmetry.

proportion_preservation_in_special_proje.general_rule: A 3D→2D projection does not normally preserve lengths.
proportion_preservation_in_special_proje.exception: Orthogonal projection onto a plane that contains a symmetry of the icosahedron can preserve certain linear relations.

key_property.preservation_condition: This golden-ratio relation is preserved when projecting orthogonally onto planes that contain the regular decagons of the icosahedron.

proportion_preservation_in_special_proje.importance: This preservation allows the projected decagon to serve as a reliable geometric template for constructing or analyzing the icosahedron.
